title: issues with negative array indexes
body:
Latest git build.

Negative indexes are commonly used for fixed point numbers, but in this case I was using them to store edge conditions for a loop.

If I define an unpacked array as:
```SystemVerilog
logic [WIDTH-1:-1] tbl [-1:WIDTH-1];
```
Than I am unable to use negative indexes in an assignment pattern:
```SystemVerilog
tbl = '{-1: '1, default: 'd1};
```
I get the error:
```sh
%Error: ../../rtl/...sv:72:19: syntax error, unexpected ':', expecting ',' or '}'
   72 |         tbl = '{-1: '1, default: 'd1};
```

It also seems that checking whether an index is within range is not done at least in some cases (`for (i=0; i<N; i++)` loop and index `[i-1]`). But I would have to rewrite some code before I can reproduce it.
